steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states directly: authorship of No. 62 is hard to determine ‘not from a lack of evidence but from too much contradictory evidence.’ The correct choice must reflect this specific reason.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Support can be found for a strong case that either Harrington or Mercer was the sole author of “The Senate.”": contradictory evidence pointing to both candidates is exactly why determination is difficult. ✓
  • "Evidence shows that two versions of “The Senate,” one authored by Harrington and another authored by Mercer, appeared in different New York newspapers.": the passage never mentions separate versions. This is an invented detail. ✗
  • "The evidence that would indisputably prove that either Harrington or Mercer was the sole author of “The Senate” has been lost.": the passage explicitly says the problem is not a lack of evidence. This directly contradicts the text. ✗
  • "It is most likely that “The Senate” was the product of a collaborative effort between Harrington and Mercer.": the either-or framing in the passage does not suggest collaboration. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
